summary_one_line: Input must form a valid HTTP-like response whose headers parse and complete so non-header body bytes (nread>0) reach the body-write branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must encode a recognizable HTTP/protocol structure (a magic URL scheme like 'pop3://' or '://127.0.0.1:9001' plus an 'HTTP/' status/header block ending in CRLF) so that Curl_http_readwrite_headers consumes and finishes the header phase, clearing k->header, and leaves at least one non-header body byte (nread>0), satisfying both conjuncts of !k->header && (nread > 0 || is_empty_data).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry intact literal protocol tokens at fixed offsets: 'pop3' / 'pop' at 0x06, '://' near 0x0a-0x0c, the '127.0.0.1:9001'-style host:port run, and a contiguous 'GHTTP/ me@home...' header block with CRLF line endings, so the parser advances past headers and a body byte remains.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35-73 bytes) and replace those literal tokens with '%'/repeated-fill noise (e.g. '%`%a>f', '%%%%' runs, '0I..Q.GH.+'), so the HTTP/protocol header block never parses to completion, k->header stays set, and the true side of line 666 is never taken (L hitcount=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes intercepted CMP operands (the literal scheme/'HTTP/' bytes the curl header parser compares against) into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the exact protocol literals naive/value_profile cannot brute-force; the value_profile arm lacks I2SRandReplace and its distance-bucket feedback alone is insufficient, so only the I2S-bearing arm of each pair resolves.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the conjunction requiring no-header state plus a body byte.
Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique only.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile, also an I2S delta.
Both winners resolve all 10 trials while both losers block all 10, with zero hitcount on the loser side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=341  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The branch sits after the header-parsing block, so reaching its true side depends on header parsing finishing.
Winner seeds contain intact protocol literals: a pop3 scheme, '://' and a host:port, plus an HTTP header block.
A winner seed carries a contiguous GHTTP header block with a To: header and CRLF.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and dominated by '%' fill noise rather than protocol literals.
At the body-write offsets the winner concentrates on literal 'HTTP/' bytes (G,H,T,T,P,/) while losers scatter '%'/noise.
The loser-blocking seeds never take the true side of the blocker (true counts all zero on L).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes an intercepted CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so its arm cannot synthesize the literals.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 430 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00f515acb6373e33`; the chain (21 levels) reports 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 19, 20' (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only), the lower-bound I2S signal for a cmplog-discovered winner under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same post-header body-write gate where naive/value_profile resolve the true branch as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), showing the literal protocol-token substitution is not what unblocks line 666.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP operands I2S substitutes are inferred from the curl HTTP/protocol parser path rather than directly read from a CmpLogObserver dump, so the specific compared literal is presumed not proven.
